name: Story Title
title: Story Title | From Hindu Scriptures
subtitle: Ancient wisdom tale
description: A beautiful story from Hindu scriptures that teaches important moral and spiritual lessons. Describe the story's context and what readers will learn (100-500 characters).
significance: Explain the spiritual lesson and why this story is important in Hindu tradition.

seo.title: Story Title - Hindu Mythology Tale with Moral
seo.description: Read this beautiful story from Hindu scriptures with moral lesson. Ancient wisdom tale for spiritual growth.

sections.title: Introduction
sections.content: Introduction to the story, setting the scene, introducing the main characters, and preparing the reader for the narrative. Mention the scripture source (Mahabharata, Ramayana, Purana, etc.).

sections.title: The Story
sections.content:
Once upon a time, in the ancient kingdom of [Place], there lived [Main Character]...

[Write the complete story here with vivid details, dialogues, and narrative flow. Include:
- Setting and characters
- The conflict or situation
- The events that unfold
- The climax
- The resolution

Make it engaging while staying true to the original scripture. Use proper storytelling techniques with clear beginning, middle, and end.]
sections.summary: Brief summary of the story plot
sections.moral: The main moral or spiritual lesson from this story

sections.title: Moral and Spiritual Lessons
sections.content:
Detailed explanation of the lessons taught by this story:

1. **Primary Lesson**: Explain the main moral teaching
2. **Secondary Lessons**: Other wisdom teachings embedded in the story
3. **Practical Application**: How to apply these lessons in modern life
4. **Spiritual Significance**: Deeper spiritual meaning for devotees

sections.title: Related Shlokas
sections.sanskrit:
यदा यदा हि धर्मस्य ग्लानिर्भवति भारत।
अभ्युत्थानमधर्मस्य तदात्मानं सृजाम्यहम्॥
sections.english: Whenever there is a decline in righteousness and rise of unrighteousness, O Bharata, then I manifest myself.
sections.meaning: The Lord incarnates whenever dharma declines and adharma rises to establish righteousness again.
sections.transliteration:
Yada yada hi dharmasya glanir bhavati bharata
Abhyuthanam adharmasya tadatmanam srujamyaham

sections.title: Source and Origin
sections.content: Information about which Hindu scripture this story comes from, the historical context, and its significance in the text.

sections.title: Cultural Significance
sections.content: How this story has influenced Hindu culture, art, festivals, and daily life. Mention any temples, celebrations, or customs associated with this narrative.
